allOutcomes.title: Worsening HF events (Peto HR from counts)

evidence.label: Co-Primary: KCCQ-CSS
evidence.source: Kosiborod MN et al. NEJM 2023;389:1069-1084, Table 2
evidence.text: Change from baseline in KCCQ-CSS at 52 weeks: semaglutide +16.6 vs placebo +8.7 points (ETD 7.8; 95% CI 3.4 to 12.2; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Co-Primary: Body Weight
evidence.source: Kosiborod MN et al. NEJM 2023;389:1069-1084, Table 2
evidence.text: Body weight change at 52 weeks: semaglutide -13.3% vs placebo -2.6% (ETD -10.7%; 95% CI -11.9 to -9.4; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Worsening HF Events
evidence.text: Worsening HF events: 1 (0.4%) with semaglutide vs 12 (4.5%) with placebo.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female, age above or equal to 18 years at the time of signing informed consent.~* Body mass index (BMI) greater than or equal to 30.0 kg/m^2~* New York Heart Association (NYHA) Class II-IV~* Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) greater than or equal to 45 percentage at screening~Exclusion Criteria:~* A self-reported change in body weight greater than 5 kg (11 lbs) within 90 days before screening irrespective of medical records~* Haemoglobin A1c (HbA1c) greate...

allOutcomes.title: Worsening HF events (Peto HR from counts)

evidence.label: Co-Primary: KCCQ-CSS
evidence.source: Kosiborod MN et al. NEJM 2024;390:1394-1407, Table 2
evidence.text: KCCQ-CSS at 52 weeks: ETD 7.3 points (95% CI 4.1 to 10.4; P<0.001). Consistent benefit across pre-specified subgroups.

evidence.label: Co-Primary: Body Weight
evidence.source: Kosiborod MN et al. NEJM 2024;390:1394-1407, Table 2
evidence.text: Body weight: ETD -6.4% (95% CI -7.6 to -5.2; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female, age above or equal to 18 years at the time of signing informed consent.~* Body mass index (BMI) greater than or equal to 30.0 kg/m^2~* New York Heart Association (NYHA) Class II-IV~* Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) greater than or equal to 45% at screening~* Diagnosed with T2D greater than or equal to 90 days prior to the day of screening~* HbA1c of below or equal to 10.0% as measured at the screening visit~Exclusion Criteria:~* A self-reported c...

evidence.label: Primary: CV Death or WHF
evidence.source: Packer M et al. NEJM 2024 (SUMMIT), Table 2
evidence.text: CV death or worsening HF: HR 0.62 (95% CI 0.41-0.95; P=0.026). Events: 36 (9.9%) vs 56 (15.3%).

evidence.label: KCCQ-CSS
evidence.text: KCCQ-CSS change at 52 weeks: ETD 6.9 points (95% CI 3.3 to 10.6; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Body Weight & 6MWD
evidence.text: Body weight: ETD -11.6% (95% CI -12.9 to -10.4). 6MWD: ETD 18.3 m (95% CI 9.9 to 26.7). Both P<0.001.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 40 Years; healthy volunteers: f. title: RapidMeta Cardiology | Incretin Therapies in HFpEF Ultra-Precision v12.5
